title: Skin cancer surveillance

examination.text: Supports documenting concerns, personalized follow-up context, clinician-performed full skin and regional lymph-node examination, dermoscopy, medical photography, and completed specialist follow-up without generating an interval or scan.
examination.rationale: The exact melanoma follow-up section supports documenting a skin-cancer surveillance context, duration, symptoms and concerns, clinician skin inspection, lesion distribution, general appearance, available records, pathology results and clinician-arranged follow-up without asserting recurrence.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting concerns, personalized follow-up context, clinician-performed full skin and regional lymph-node examination, dermoscopy, medical photography, and completed specialist follow-up without generating an interval or scan.

investigations.text: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.
investigations.rationale: The exact imaging section supports documenting reviewed clinical photographs when available and consented; no comparison finding is invented.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

sources_used.title: Melanoma: assessment and management — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE skin-cancer pathway adaptation

source_limitations: Cancer type and stage, treatment history, exact surveillance schedule, complete skin and node findings, dermoscopy, imaging, pathology interpretation, recurrence status, biopsy or excision, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
